findings: Features e23, e24, and e25 exhibit weak shortcut-like predictive leverage consistent with their near-constant low cardinality values.
Low cardinality features across e23 to e32 show limited predictive signal individually, preserving unresolved tension with class-conditioned structural variation.
No strong shortcut signals were detected in the tested features, indicating limited shortcut risk from these low cardinality features.
contradictions: Tension between near-constant low cardinality features and other features with strong class-conditioned variation remains unresolved locally.
limitations: Only a subset of features (e23, e24, e25) were tested individually due to budget constraints; combined feature shortcut leverage was not tested.
